questionHtml: Outline how a protein is made in a cell by the process of translation.
markschemeHtml:
1. translation occurs on ribosomes when proteins/polypeptides are synthesized;
  2. amino acid sequence of a protein is determined by the mRNA;
  3. mRNA is determined by the order of bases of DNA/order of bases sequence in a gene;
  4. codons (of 3 bases) on mRNA correspond to one amino acid in a polypeptide;
  5. codons are on mRNA and anticodons on tRNA;
  6. mRNA binds to ribosome/(during initiation) small ribosomal subunit binds to the start of the mRNA sequence;
  7. tRNA transfers an amino acid to large ribosomal subunit/to the ribosome;
  8. reference to start or stop codon;
  9. tRNA moves to the next mRNA codon to continue the process, creating an amino acid chain;
  10. peptide bond formed between amino acids
  11. translation/order of amino acids depends on complementary base pairing between codons and anticodons;

meta.title: Outline how a protein is made in a cell by the process of translation.
